chunks.text: Daniel Grubb and Ruocheng Wang win EE140/240A Keysight design competition - EECS at Berkeley Daniel Grubb (EE140) and Ruocheng Wang (EE240A) have won an Analog Integrated Circuits I class design competition sponsored by Keysight technologies. The students designed low-power and high-speed LCD display drivers for a smartwatch display for the classes taught by Assistant Prof. Rikky Muller. Competition finalists gave presentations to a panel of judges that included three Berkeley alumni who are now Keysight engineers. Grubb and Wang won hand-held digital multimeters generously donated by Keysight. Categories Alumni Education Honors Industry Research Students View Open Faculty Positions
